text:
A person at a trial for damages may not refer to or offer as evidence of the negligence or due care of a party:

(1) an action taken by the department under this chapter;

(2) the findings on which that action is based; or

(3) the security or evidence of financial responsibility filed under this chapter.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 601. Motor Vehicle Safety Responsibility Act
##### § 601.005 — Evidence in Civil Suit

A person at a trial for damages may not refer to or offer as evidence of the negligence or due care of a party:

    (1) an action taken by the department under this chapter;

    (2) the findings on which that action is based; or

    (3) the security or evidence of financial responsibility filed under this chapter.

*Acts 2017, 85th Leg., R.S., Ch. 533 (S.B.312), Sec. 50, eff. September 1, 2017.*

subsections.text: A person at a trial for damages may not refer to or offer as evidence of the negligence or due care of a party:
an action taken by the department under this chapter;
the findings on which that action is based; or
the security or evidence of financial responsibility filed under this chapter.
